revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23987730427|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[en:Mausoleum of Emperor Tenji]][[ja:御廟野古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name=Gobyono Kofun |location={{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}{{ill|Yamashina-ku, Kyoto|en|Yamashina-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q1076993|lt=Yamashina Ward, Kyoto|1=Yamashina-ku, Kyoto}}御陵上御廟野町 |coordinates={{coordinates|34|59|50.91|N|135|48|25.19|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=御廟野古墳（伝天智天皇陵）}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, broadly considered an {{ill|Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳|lt=Imperial mound|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |built=Late {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|qid=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} - {{ill|8th century|en|8th century|qid=Q8086|lt=8th century|1=8th century}} |map_size=Upper octagon: diameter approx. 46m; Lower square: diameter approx. 70m; Height 8m |Occupant=({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} Historic Site) {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|qid=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}} (38th Emperor) |burial goods=None |Mausoleum={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} Historic Site1 "Yamashina no Misasagi" (山科陵) |image=[[File:Gobyono Kofun, haisho-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun front view
（{{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|qid=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}} Yamashina Mausoleum viewing area） |map={{location map|Japan Kyoto|coordinates=34|coordinates=59|coordinates=50.91|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=25.19|map_dot_label=Gobyono Kofun|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.svg|marksize=28|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}}| }} '''Gobyono Kofun''' (御廟野古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Yamashina-ku, Kyoto|en|Yamashina-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q1076993|lt=Yamashina Ward, Kyoto|1=Yamashina-ku, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, Japan. The tumulus is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, dating to the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has Historic Site1 it as "'''Yamashina no Misasagi''' (山科陵)" and attributes it as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|qid=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}}, the 38th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == [[File:Mausoleum of Emperor Tenji aerial photo 2020.jpg|thumb|Aerial view (2020)]] Gobyono Kofun is unique in that both the historical evidence of the buried individual and its Historic Site as an imperial tomb are largely undisputed, making it among the rare tumuli that can confidently be referred to as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|qid=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}}. Only the {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Ouka Tomb|ja|野口王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Ouka Tomb|qq=}} (the combined tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q335181}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|qid=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō|1=Empress Jitō}}) shares this level of certainty. Furthermore, the {{ill|Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳|lt=Imperial mound|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} type used in modern imperial tombs, beginning with the {{ill|Fushimi Momoyama Mausoleum|lt=Fushimi Momoyama Mausoleum|lt=Fushimi Momoyama Tomb|ja|伏見桃山陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Momoyama Tomb|qq=}} of {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|qid=Q174231|lt=Emperor Meiji|1=Emperor Meiji}}, was modeled after Gobyono Kofun as it was interpreted during the {{ill|Taisho built|ja|大正時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taisho built|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大正|1=Taishō era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q329174}}. The construction period of Gobyono Kofun is estimated to be the late 7th to early 8th century. As an {{ill|Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳|lt=Imperial mound|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, it measures approximately 46 meters in diameter at the upper octagonal section, 70 meters in diameter at the lower square section, and reaches a height of 8 meters. By the mid-7th century, octagonal kofun became a unique burial typeat reserved for sovereigns. Known octagonal kofun include {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (current tomb of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|qid=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei|1=Emperor Jomei}}) in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|qid=Q127026|lt=Sakurai, Nara|1=Sakurai, Nara}}, {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|en|Kengoshizuka Kofun|qid=Q11571102|lt=Kengoshizuka Kofun|1=Kengoshizuka Kofun}} (possibly the tomb of {{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|en|Empress Kōgyoku|qid=Q235296|lt=Empress Saimei|1=Empress Kōgyoku}}), {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Ouka Tomb|ja|野口王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Ouka Tomb|qq=}} (current tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q335181}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|qid=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō|1=Empress Jitō}}), and {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|en|Nakaoyama Kofun|qid=Q11363940|lt=Nakaoyama Kofun|1=Nakaoyama Kofun}} (possibly the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|qid=Q357246|lt=Emperor Monmu|1=Emperor Monmu}}) in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|qid=Q752397|lt=Asuka, Nara|1=Asuka, Nara}}. Gobyono Kofun represents the earliest emergence of a uniquely imperial burial. Additional examples include {{ill|Tsukamyojin Kofun|ja|束明神古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukamyojin Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|en|Prince Kusakabe|qid=Q1046438|lt=Prince Kusakabe|1=Prince Kusakabe}}) and {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|en|Iwayayama Kofun|qid=Q11474389|lt=Iwayayama Kofun|1=Iwayayama Kofun}}, which may also feature octagonal mounds. == Rituals == Historically, Gobyono Kofun has been highly significant. It was listed among the ten "nearby tombs" in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|qid=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}. During the {{ill|Enthronement of the Japanese emperor|en|Enthronement of the Japanese emperor|qid=Q4202178|lt=Enthronement of the Japanese emperor|1=Enthronement of the Japanese emperor}} in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, a court envoy (''chokushi'') presented offerings (''hōhei'') at this tomb.Tokoro Isao, "Compilation of Fundamental Historical Materials on Modern Imperial Ceremonies," p. 38. A sundial, symbolizing Emperor Tenji’s contributions to timekeeping, remains installed near the entrance to the approach marked by an official Imperial Household Agency plaque. == Access == * {{ill|Keihan Bus|en|Keihan Bus|qid=Q11375813|lt=Keihan Bus|1=Keihan Bus}} Stop: "Ryo-ga-oka Emperor Tenji Mausoleum" (陵ヶ岡天智天皇陵) - Direct access. == notes == == External Links == {{Commonscat|Mausoleum of Emperor Tenji}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/038/index.html Yamashina Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} {{Kofun navbar}} }} {{translated page|ja|御廟野古墳|version=96035658|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:御廟野古墳]] {{Commons category|Mausoleum of Emperor Tenji}} [[en:Mausoleum of Emperor Tenji]] {{wikidata link|Q11489010}} [[Category:Emperor Tenji]] [[Category:History of Yamashina-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Octagonal Kofun]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
